channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Property developers investing in 'farms for children', childcare report finds
item.description: For-profit providers have been accused of putting growth and revenue ahead of quality care in a report into childcare safety failures.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: A NSW parliamentary inquiry has linked the influence of real estate developers and investors to a lack of services in less-affluent suburbs.

item.title: Last two bodies of Italian cave divers recovered
item.description: Finnish divers have recovered the remaining bodies of the divers that died in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

media:group.media:description: Two other bodies were located on Tuesday.

item.title: Putin's China visit looked like Trump's but couldn't have been more different
item.description: Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping agreed to a raft of deals in front of invited press less than a week after Donald Trump left China's capital with no confirmed deals.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fang and Patrick Martin

media:group.media:description: Vladimir Putin met Xi Jinping in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Truck torn in half after collision with train at level crossing, six injured
item.description: Two toddlers and four women are injured after a train collided with a truck at a level crossing in Perth's east.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Mayes

media:group.media:description: A Midland-bound train crosses a pedestrian walkway in Guildford. (File photo)

item.title: Brothers of Bondi hero ordered to stay away from him after alleged threat
item.description: Two brothers of Ahmed Al Ahmed faced Bankstown Local Court over alleged verbal threats via the phone.

media:group.media:description: Ahmed Al Ahmed's brothers have been ordered to stay away from him after they allegedly verbally threatened him over the phone.

item.title: New move to change SA's abortion laws goes before state parliament
item.description: South Australia's parliament is set to debate restricting abortion access for the third time in less than two years, with One Nation's breakthrough election result meaning the laws could pass the upper house.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall and Kathryn Bermingham

media:group.media:description: Upper house MP Sarah Game is introducing legislation in SA parliament that would tighten abortion restrictions.

item.title: New Qld public hospital to refuse some services for religious reasons
item.description: The Queensland premier has opened the state's first major new public hospital in almost a decade — at Springfield, south-west of Brisbane — but it won't offer some services for religious reasons.
item.dc:creator: Emma Pollard and Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Mater Hospital Springfield is a 186-bed facility expected to care for around 185,000 patients each year.

item.title: San Diego shooters named Australia’s worst mass murderer in online posts
item.description: Thousands of kilometres away, but the terrible connection to Australia is evident. ABC NEWS Verify looks at the inspiration behind the San Diego Islamic Centre murders.
item.dc:creator: Michael Workman

media:group.media:description: People embracing near the scene of a shooting.

item.title: Lawyers say teen accused of trying to hijack plane was mentally impaired
item.description: A teen who allegedly tried to hijack a plane at Avalon Airport felt relieved when passengers restrained him, describing it as a "weight off his shoulders", his lawyers say.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Passengers restrain a person onboard a Jetstar flight from Avalon Airport in March last year.

item.title: Inside the 'worst managed infrastructure project in Australian history'
item.description: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.
item.dc:creator: Tom Hartley and Joanna Woodburn

media:group.media:description: Everald Compton isn't giving up on the Inland Rail project.

item.title: Former Nationals candidate refused bail over alleged hit and run
item.description: The 26-year-old allegedly struck the victim with his Toyota Hilux as he was leaving for work, causing 'catastrophic' head injuries.
item.dc:creator: David Weber

media:group.media:description: Police on Marri Drive early last Wednesday.

item.title: Thailand tightens visa rules for nearly 100 countries including Australia
item.description: Thailand is reducing the visa-free period available to visitors from 90 countries, including Australia, with the duration decided on a country-by-country basis.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Silva

media:group.media:description: The new changes will end Thailand's 60-day visa-free stay for tourists from 93 countries.

item.title: Banks, telcos briefed on threat of 'dangerous' AI model
item.description: A new generation of AI models deemed "too dangerous" for public release is driving Australia's efforts to lure top AI companies to establish a major presence in the country.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Wilson

media:group.media:description: Access to cutting edge AI models deemed "too dangerous" for public release has become a new motivation in Australia's efforts to lure Anthropic to set up major operations here.

item.title: Man killed mother with tool while under 'supernatural' attack, court hears
item.description: An Adelaide Hills man standing trial for the murder of his mother describes to the Supreme Court how he repeatedly struck her while he was panicked and "under attack" by "supernatural beings". Warning: This story contains images and details that may be distressing to some readers.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Linda Simon was murdered in 2022.

item.title: Paedophile former magistrate to be released on home detention
item.description: Paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy will be released from jail when his sentence expires next month, despite an ongoing application to have him indefinitely detained.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy was jailed in 2001 for the sexual abuse of four children.

item.title: Bingo player awarded $285k after tripping on cracked tile at Canberra club
item.description: The woman, who was awarded $285,000, rated her pain as 10 out of 10 and spent three nights in hospital.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: The woman fell at the Hellenic Club of Canberra in Phillip in November 2020.

item.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise
item.description: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange, Callum Liddelow, Tim Little, Eugene Boisvert, and Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Health officials are urging the public to get vaccinated against diphtheria, particularly those in high-risk communities.

item.title: Alcaraz injury woes deepen, former champion out of Wimbledon
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.

media:group.media:description: Carlos Alcaraz is not ready to take to the court yet, aiming to return for the US hardcourt season.

item.title: Death of Peter Hollingworth sparks difficult feelings for abuse victim
item.description: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth, who resigned over his handling of child abuse in the church, died in Melbourne on Tuesday at the age of 91.
item.dc:creator: Ned Hammond and Will Murray

media:group.media:description: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth died on Tuesday, aged 91.

item.title: Australia's costliest disaster recovery program fails to deliver a single home
item.description: A new report finds a disaster recovery program set up after the devastating 2022 floods in northern NSW is yet to deliver any of the homes it promised.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ross

media:group.media:description: More than 2,000 homes across northern NSW were declared uninhabitable after devastating floods hit the region in early 2022.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Dick

media:group.media:description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has comforted Kumanjayi Little Baby's family in Alice Springs.

item.title: Pensioner offered cash for amputation weeks before death, court told
item.description: Witnesses have given evidence about a 66-year-old Queensland man shopping around for someone in his town to amputate his leg in the weeks before he died at a public park.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: The court heard John Yalu's co-workers told him not to accept Kalman Tal's offer.

item.title: Teenaged trio arrested over Melbourne nightclub arsons
item.description: Victoria Police allege the three males are responsible for arson attacks at nightclubs La Di Da and Electric Bar earlier this month.

media:group.media:description: Police have arrested three people today over alleged arson attacks at Melbourne nightlife venues.

item.title: Maroons hit with triple injury blow as Blues look for whitewash
item.description: Injuries to three key players have compounded Queensland's task to avoid a 3-0 women's State of Origin whitewash.

media:group.media:description: Queensland skipper Tamika Upton is out of Origin III.

item.title: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia and so is misinformation about it
item.description: Falsehoods about diphtheria are spreading online as a worsening outbreak of the disease affects Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Carter

media:group.media:description: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia, but so are falsehoods about the highly contagious disease.
